subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 6
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For gridded questions, enter just the number. For fill-in-the-blank equations, enter the number that makes the equation true.

questions.questionText: What multiplication fact does this array of dots show?
questions.explanation: There are 6 rows of 3 dots: 6 × 3 = 18.

questions.questionText: Which number is shown by these base-ten blocks?
questions.explanation: 3 hundreds, 0 tens, and 7 ones make 307.

questions.questionText: What fraction of this bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 4 equal parts are shaded, so 3/4.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 2 of the 6 equal parts are shaded, so 2/6.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is between 7 and 8 and the minute hand points to 9, which is 45 minutes, so 7:45.

questions.questionText: What is 526 − 184? Enter just the number.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
What is 42 ÷ 6?
Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: 1/4 has fewer shaded parts than 3/4, so 1/4 < 3/4.

questions.questionText: How many sides does this shape have?
questions.explanation: A hexagon has 6 sides.

questions.questionText: How many cans did Ravi collect?
questions.explanation: Ravi's bar reaches 15 on the scale.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 3 equal parts and the point is at the first mark, which is 1/3.

questions.questionText: What fraction of this bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 8 equal parts are shaded, so 3/8.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
A baker puts 48 rolls equally into 6 baskets. How many rolls are in each basket? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 rolls in each basket.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
questions.explanation: 2/4 covers the same amount as 1/2, so they are equivalent.

questions.questionText: Art class starts at the time shown and lasts 40 minutes. What time does it end?
questions.explanation: 1:20 plus 40 minutes is 2:00.

questions.questionText: A poster is 9 inches long and 4 inches wide. What is its area, in square inches? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: Area = 9 × 4 = 36 square inches.

questions.questionText: Each star stands for 4 stickers earned. How many stickers did Group C earn?
questions.explanation: 6 stars × 4 = 24 stickers.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Select all the shapes that are quadrilaterals (shapes with exactly 4 sides).
questions.explanation: A rectangle and a trapezoid each have exactly 4 sides. A hexagon has 6 sides and a triangle has 3.

questions.questionText: Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: With the same denominator, fewer shaded parts means less: 2/8 < 5/8.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 6 equal parts and the point is at the fourth mark, which is 4/6.

questions.questionText: There are 10 marbles in a bag. 7 of them are blue. What fraction of the marbles are blue?
questions.explanation: 7 out of 10 marbles are blue, so 7/10.

questions.questionText: There are 24 crayons in a box. 1/4 of them are red. How many crayons are red? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: 1/4 of 24 means 24 ÷ 4 = 6 red crayons.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 40 ÷ 8 = 5, so the blank is 8.

questions.questionText: Which expression has the same value as 8 × 5?
questions.explanation: 8 × 5 means five 8s added together: 8 + 8 + 8 + 8 + 8 = 40.

questions.questionText: In the counting pattern 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, ..., select all the numbers that belong in the pattern.
questions.explanation: The pattern is multiples of 6. 42 and 48 are multiples of 6; 40 and 45 are not.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 7 × 8 = 56, so the blank is 56.

questions.questionText: A farmer planted 6 rows of corn with 8 plants in each row. Then 15 plants were eaten by deer. How many plants are left?
questions.explanation: 6 × 8 = 48 plants; 48 − 15 = 33 plants left.

questions.questionText: Each notebook costs $6. Marco buys 4 notebooks and pays with a $40 bill. How much change does he get back?
A rectangle has an area of 63 square meters. If one side is 9 meters long, how long is the other side, in meters? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: Area = length × width, so 63 ÷ 9 = 7 meters.

questions.questionText: What is the perimeter, in units, of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 7 + 3 + 7 + 3 = 20 units. (Area would be 21, a common trap.)

questions.questionText: Lunch started at 12:40 and ended at 1:15. How long did lunch last?
questions.explanation: From 12:40 to 1:00 is 20 minutes, and 1:00 to 1:15 is 15 minutes: 20 + 15 = 35 minutes.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is the greatest?
questions.explanation: When the top number is 1, the smaller the bottom number, the larger the fraction. 1/3 is the greatest because 3 is the smallest denominator.

questions.questionText: Select all the expressions that equal 36.
Select all the fractions that are equal to 1/3.
questions.explanation: 2/6, 3/9, and 4/12 each equal 1/3. 2/5 is more than 1/3.

questions.questionText: Select all the numbers that round to 400 when rounded to the nearest hundred.
questions.explanation: 352 and 438 each round to 400. 349 rounds to 300 and 451 rounds to 500.

questions.questionText: A store had 8 boxes of pencils with 9 pencils in each box. They sold 31 pencils. How many pencils are left? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: 8 × 9 = 72 pencils; 72 − 31 = 41 left.
